title: ai-review.sh prefers stale release binary over the freshly-built debug binary
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/scripts/ai-review.sh b/scripts/ai-review.sh
--- a/scripts/ai-review.sh
+++ b/scripts/ai-review.sh
@@ -221,12 +221,9 @@ fi
 
 # --- reviewer client = freshly-built mu, never the (possibly stale) installed one
-# Prefer the DEBUG binary: it is the one the build line above just
-# refreshed. Preferring release here once handed the panel a weeks-old
-# release build that lacked a flag the script passed — every reviewer
-# died at clap with UNCLEAR (2026-06-11). Release is only a fallback.
 cargo build --bin mu -q 2>/dev/null || true
-if   [ -x ./target/debug/mu ];   then MU=./target/debug/mu
-elif [ -x ./target/release/mu ]; then MU=./target/release/mu
+if   [ -x ./target/release/mu ]; then MU=./target/release/mu
+elif [ -x ./target/debug/mu ];   then MU=./target/debug/mu
 else MU="$(command -v mu || true)"; fi
 [ -n "${MU:-}" ] || { echo "${C_RED}ai-review: no mu binary found${C_OFF}" >&2; exit 2; }

title: Script rebuilds the debug artifact but runs the possibly-stale release one
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/run-tool.sh b/run-tool.sh
new file mode 100755
--- /dev/null
+++ b/run-tool.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Build a fresh DEBUG binary so we always test the current source,
+# then invoke it with the brand-new --json flag.
+cargo build --bin tool >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+
+# Pick the binary to run.
+if   [ -x ./target/release/tool ]; then BIN=./target/release/tool
+elif [ -x ./target/debug/tool ];   then BIN=./target/debug/tool
+else BIN="$(command -v tool)"; fi
+
+# --json was added today; an older cached release build lacks it and
+# will abort at argument parsing.
+exec "$BIN" --json "$@"

title: SessionEventLog::append reserves id outside the disk-writer lock, racing the ordered command-append path
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.

title: Append-only log reserves sequence id outside the mutex, so disk/slice order can drift from id order
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/eventlog/eventlog.go b/eventlog/eventlog.go
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/eventlog/eventlog.go
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+package eventlog
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"sync"
+	"sync/atomic"
+)
+
+// Log is an append-only event log mirrored to a JSONL-ish file. Each
+// record gets a monotonic id; consumers replay the file and the
+// in-memory slice and assume both are sorted by id.
+type Log struct {
+	mu     sync.Mutex
+	nextID atomic.Uint64
+	file   *os.File
+	events []string
+}
+
+// AppendCommand is the strict path: it holds mu across id assignment,
+// the disk write, and the in-memory push, so file order and slice
+// order always match id order.
+func (l *Log) AppendCommand(payload string) uint64 {
+	l.mu.Lock()
+	defer l.mu.Unlock()
+	id := l.nextID.Add(1)
+	line := fmt.Sprintf("%d:%s\n", id, payload)
+	_, _ = l.file.WriteString(line)
+	l.events = append(l.events, line)
+	return id
+}
+
+// Append is the best-effort path. It reserves an id WITHOUT the lock,
+// then takes the lock separately to write and push.
+func (l *Log) Append(payload string) uint64 {
+	id := l.nextID.Add(1)
+	line := fmt.Sprintf("%d:%s\n", id, payload)
+	l.mu.Lock()
+	_, _ = l.file.WriteString(line)
+	l.events = append(l.events, line)
+	l.mu.Unlock()
+	return id
+}

title: ai-review.sh drops the /api/ps pre-flight and blindly targets the configured ollama model
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/scripts/ai-review.sh b/scripts/ai-review.sh
--- a/scripts/ai-review.sh
+++ b/scripts/ai-review.sh
@@ -255,37 +255,7 @@ if   [ -x ./target/debug/mu ];   then MU=./target/debug/mu
 elif [ -x ./target/release/mu ]; then MU=./target/release/mu
 else MU="$(command -v mu || true)"; fi
 [ -n "${MU:-}" ] || { echo "${C_RED}ai-review: no mu binary found${C_OFF}" >&2; exit 2; }
 
-# ── Local-reviewer pre-flight: never trigger an ollama model reload ─────────
-# The local ollama reviewer (primary-1 in single-shot, the per-commit leaf in
-# chunked) reads $PROVIDER/$MODEL — so resolving them here covers both modes.
-# A cold load of the 262k reviewer is minutes, and that reload has SIGTERM'd
-# reviewers mid-stream before (see the MU_REVIEW_TIMEOUT note above). So decide
-# from ollama's /api/ps what's actually resident:
-#   - same model already loaded  -> run local (no delay)
-#   - box reachable but empty     -> run local (a load evicts nobody)
-#   - a DIFFERENT model loaded    -> fall back (don't evict it / eat the reload)
-#   - ollama unreachable          -> fall back (can't run local against a dead box)
-# Match is tag-tolerant (ollama reports ':latest'). Synthesis (SYNTH_*) is
-# hosted by default and is not checked here.
-ensure_local_reviewer_loaded() {
-  [ "$PROVIDER" = ollama ] || return 0
-  local base want body loaded shown
-  base="${OLLAMA_API_BASE:-http://<ollama-host>}"
-  want="$MODEL"; case "$want" in *:*) : ;; *) want="$want:latest" ;; esac
-  if ! body="$(curl -s --max-time 5 "$base/api/ps" 2>/dev/null)"; then
-    echo "${C_DIM}ai-review: ollama unreachable at $base; primary-1 -> $FALLBACK_PROVIDER/$FALLBACK_MODEL.${C_OFF}" >&2
-    PROVIDER="$FALLBACK_PROVIDER"; MODEL="$FALLBACK_MODEL"; return 0
-  fi
-  loaded="$(printf '%s' "$body" | grep -o '"name":"[^"]*"' | sed 's/^"name":"//; s/"$//')"
-  [ -z "$loaded" ] && return 0                      # reachable + empty -> safe load
-  printf '%s\n' "$loaded" | grep -qxF "$want" && return 0   # same model resident
-  shown="${loaded//$'\n'/, }"
-  echo "${C_DIM}ai-review: ollama has a different model resident at $base (loaded: $shown); primary-1 -> $FALLBACK_PROVIDER/$FALLBACK_MODEL to avoid an eviction/reload.${C_OFF}" >&2
-  PROVIDER="$FALLBACK_PROVIDER"; MODEL="$FALLBACK_MODEL"
-}
-ensure_local_reviewer_loaded
-
 if [ -n "$TOOLS" ]; then
   TOOL_CLAUSE="Use the read and grep tools to inspect surrounding code when a judgement needs it."
 else

title: Reviewer launcher requests its model without checking what is resident, forcing an eviction with no fallback
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/scripts/run-reviewer.sh b/scripts/run-reviewer.sh
new file mode 100755
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/run-reviewer.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Run the local CI reviewer against the ollama box.
+OLLAMA="${OLLAMA_API_BASE:-http://<ollama-host>}"
+MODEL="${REVIEW_MODEL:-qwen3-coder:30b}"
+FALLBACK="${REVIEW_FALLBACK:-openai/gpt-5.5}"
+DIFF_FILE="$1"
+
+run_reviewer() {
+  # Send the diff to the configured model. ollama keeps at most one model
+  # resident; requesting a model that is not the loaded one cold-loads it
+  # (minutes for a big reviewer) and SIGTERMs whatever was streaming.
+  curl -s "$OLLAMA/api/generate" \
+    -d "{\"model\":\"$MODEL\",\"prompt\":$(jq -Rs . < "$DIFF_FILE")}"
+}
+
+# Always run locally with the configured model.
+run_reviewer

diff:
diff --git a/crates/mu-core/src/route_catalog.rs b/crates/mu-core/src/route_catalog.rs
--- a/crates/mu-core/src/route_catalog.rs
+++ b/crates/mu-core/src/route_catalog.rs
@@ -148,6 +148,11 @@ impl RouteCatalog {
     /// via `mu_ai::OllamaProvider::discover_models` and passes them in
     /// here. `configured = true` for every entry, because the names
     /// only exist if the endpoint answered `/api/tags`. Context limits
+    /// use a conservative placeholder ([`OLLAMA_DEFAULT_CONTEXT`]) since
+    /// `/api/tags` doesn't report context windows; per-model enrichment
+    /// via `/api/show` is a follow-up. Pricing is `None` (local = free).
     pub fn with_ollama_models<I, S>(mut self, models: I) -> Self
     where
         I: IntoIterator<Item = S>,
@@ -167,13 +172,8 @@ impl RouteCatalog {
                 "ollama",
                 m.as_ref(),
                 true,
-                // Unknown window: /api/tags reports none. Leave it None
-                // (not a fabricated placeholder) so it can't drive
-                // compaction; a per-model models.toml entry or the catalog
-                // sync tool fills it in. Until then compaction uses the
-                // safe DEFAULT, and the meter shows no fake denominator.
-                None,
-                None,
+                OLLAMA_DEFAULT_CONTEXT,
+                OLLAMA_DEFAULT_CONTEXT,
             ));
         }
         self
@@ -220,8 +220,8 @@ fn build_entry(
     provider_kind: &str,
     model: &str,
     configured: bool,
-    context_soft: Option<u64>,

+    context_hard: u64,
 ) -> RouteEntry {
     let catalog = model_catalog::global();
     let pricing = pricing::for_model(provider_kind, model);
     let model_settings = catalog.resolve_model(model);
     let provider_settings = catalog.provider(provider_kind);
 
-    let context_soft = model_settings.context_soft_limit.or(context_soft);
-    let context_hard = model_settings.context_hard_limit.or(context_hard);
+    let context_soft = model_settings.context_soft_limit.unwrap_or(context_soft);
+    let context_hard = model_settings.context_hard_limit.unwrap_or(context_hard);
     let favorites = catalog
         .favorites_for(provider_kind, model)
         .into_iter()
@@ -305,8 +305,8 @@ fn build_entry(
         quirks: model_settings.quirks.into_iter().map(Arc::from).collect(),
         max_output_tokens: model_settings.max_output_tokens,
         favorites,
-        context_soft_limit: context_soft,
-        context_hard_limit: context_hard,
+        context_soft_limit: Some(context_soft),
+        context_hard_limit: Some(context_hard),
         valid_effort_levels: valid_effort,
         pricing_input_per_mtok: pricing.map(|p| p.input_per_mtok),
         pricing_output_per_mtok: pricing.map(|p| p.output_per_mtok),
@@ -324,6 +324,11 @@ const OPENAI_CODEX_MODELS: &[(&str, u64, u64)] = &[("gpt-5.5", 1_000_000, 1_000_000)];
 
+/// Conservative placeholder context window for ollama models, applied
+/// to both soft and hard limits. `/api/tags` doesn't report context
+/// windows; per-model `/api/show` enrichment is a follow-up. (mu-818c)
+const OLLAMA_DEFAULT_CONTEXT: u64 = 32_768;
+
 const OPENROUTER_MODELS: &[(&str, u64, u64)] = &[
     ("anthropic/claude-opus-4.7", 200_000, 1_000_000),
     ("anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6", 200_000, 200_000),

title: watch spawns commands via ungated `sh -c`, bypassing the bash policy gate
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/crates/mu-coding/src/tools/watch.rs b/crates/mu-coding/src/tools/watch.rs
--- a/crates/mu-coding/src/tools/watch.rs
+++ b/crates/mu-coding/src/tools/watch.rs
@@ -138,17 +138,15 @@
-/// Build the watched child through the SHARED bash gate (mu-qnag) and
-/// spawn it, with `kill_on_drop` so a dropped (aborted / timed-out) task
-/// kills the process. The command is validated against `mode` FIRST: a
-/// strict session rejects anything outside its allowlist with bash's own
-/// message, BEFORE any process starts — so a rejected command surfaces an
-/// error THIS turn and never parks the session on a wakeup that won't
-/// come. Returns the rejection (or a spawn failure) as a `String` so the
-/// caller can surface it directly.
+/// Spawn `command` under `sh -c`, with `kill_on_drop` so a dropped
+/// (aborted / timed-out) task kills the process. Synchronous: surfaces
+/// spawn failures to the caller immediately, before the watch is
+/// registered.
 fn spawn_command(mode: &BashMode, command: &str) -> Result<Child, String> {
-    let mut cmd = bash::build_command(mode, command)?;
-    cmd.stdin(Stdio::null())
+    tokio::process::Command::new("sh")
+        .arg("-c")
+        .arg(command)
+        .stdin(Stdio::null())
         .stdout(Stdio::piped())
         .stderr(Stdio::piped())
         .kill_on_drop(true)
         .spawn()
         .map_err(|e| format!("watch: failed to spawn command: {e}"))
 }

title: Agent loop ends the ask on an empty/reasoning-only turn instead of re-invoking
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.

title: Agent loop returns an empty reasoning-only completion as the final answer
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/agent/loop.py b/agent/loop.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1111111
--- /dev/null
+++ b/agent/loop.py
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+def run_agent(model, prompt, max_turns=10):
+    """Drive a reasoning model until it produces a final answer.
+
+    Each turn the model either calls tools (we run them and loop) or
+    returns its answer text. Reasoning models occasionally emit a
+    reasoning-only turn: no tool call and no visible text.
+    """
+    messages = [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
+    for _ in range(max_turns):
+        resp = model.complete(messages)
+        if resp.tool_calls:
+            messages.append(resp.message)
+            messages.extend(run_tools(resp.tool_calls))
+            continue
+        # No tool call -> the model is done; hand its answer back.
+        return resp.text
+    return None

title: Remove operator-wins fold in load(): alias [models] override silently shadowed by built-in

title: Catalog lookup returns first entry in key order, shadowing the operator override
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/src/catalog.rs b/src/catalog.rs
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1111111
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/catalog.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+use std::collections::BTreeMap;
+
+#[derive(Clone)]
+struct Entry {
+    /// The provider model id, e.g. "claude-opus-4-8".
+    model: String,
+    soft_limit: u32,
+}
+
+/// Merge operator overrides on top of the built-in catalog. Built-ins are keyed
+/// by canonical name (e.g. "claude_opus_4_8"); an operator keys an override
+/// under a short alias (e.g. "opus"). Both entries may carry the SAME `model`.
+fn load(built_in: BTreeMap<String, Entry>, overrides: BTreeMap<String, Entry>) -> BTreeMap<String, Entry> {
+    let mut models = built_in;
+    models.extend(overrides);
+    models
+}
+
+/// Resolve the soft limit for `model`. An operator override must win over the
+/// built-in entry for the same model.
+fn soft_limit(models: &BTreeMap<String, Entry>, model: &str) -> Option<u32> {
+    models
+        .values()
+        .find(|e| e.model == model)
+        .map(|e| e.soft_limit)
+}

title: OllamaProvider surfaces wrapped AnthropicProvider errors verbatim, leaking the 'anthropic' label
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.

title: Wrapper surfaces inner client's error verbatim, misattributing the provider
instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
diff:
diff --git a/ollama_provider.py b/ollama_provider.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ollama_provider.py
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+"""Ollama speaks the Anthropic Messages wire against the local box, so the
+ollama client composes the Anthropic client and reuses its HTTP path."""
+
+
+class AnthropicClient:
+    label = "anthropic"
+
+    def send(self, model: str) -> str:
+        # Real HTTP would go here. A mistyped model tag 404s, and the client
+        # hardcodes its own label into the error string it raises.
+        raise RuntimeError(
+            f"{self.label} returned 404: model '{model}' not found"
+        )
+
+
+class OllamaClient:
+    """Talks to the local ollama box over the Anthropic wire by wrapping
+    AnthropicClient. Its own provider label is 'ollama'."""
+
+    label = "ollama"
+
+    def __init__(self) -> None:
+        self._inner = AnthropicClient()
+
+    def send(self, model: str) -> str:
+        # Identical wire protocol; delegate wholesale to the inner client.
+        return self._inner.send(model)
